---
title: "EU-SILC Country Report"
subtitle: "Poverty & Inequality Indicators"
author: "ICON Advanced Python Course"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    theme: cosmo
    code-fold: true
    code-tools: true
    fig-align: center
    tbl-cap-location: top
  pdf:
    toc: true
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]
country = "LU"
```

```{python}
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
def find_project_root(s):
    for c in [s, *s.parents]:
        if (c / "pyproject.toml").exists():
            return c
    raise FileNotFoundError("pyproject.toml not found")
PROJECT_ROOT = find_project_root(Path().resolve())
DATA_DIR = PROJECT_ROOT / "data"
from silc_toolkit import load_incomes, arop_rate, gini_coefficient, s80s20_ratio
COUNTRY = country
NAMES = {"BE": "Belgium", "ES": "Spain", "FR": "France", "EL": "Greece",
         "HU": "Hungary", "IE": "Ireland", "LU": "Luxembourg", "SE": "Sweden"}
COUNTRY_NAME = NAMES.get(COUNTRY, COUNTRY)
records = []
for year in range(2004, 2014):
    try:
        inc = load_incomes(COUNTRY, year, DATA_DIR)
        if inc:
            inc_s = sorted(inc); n = len(inc_s)
            records.append({"Year": year, "N": n,
                "Median": int(inc_s[n // 2]),
                "AROP%": round(arop_rate(inc) * 100, 1),
                "Gini": round(gini_coefficient(inc), 4),
                "S80S20": round(s80s20_ratio(inc), 2)})
    except FileNotFoundError:
        pass
df = pd.DataFrame(records)
yr_range = f"{int(df['Year'].min())}-{int(df['Year'].max())}" if len(df) else "n/a"
```

## Overview

This report presents poverty and inequality indicators for **`{python} COUNTRY_NAME`** (`{python} COUNTRY`), covering survey years `{python} yr_range`. All figures derive from the EU-SILC Public Use File (Eurostat) and should be read with the caution attached to sample-based estimates.

::: {.callout-note}
**Indicator glossary** — **AROP%**: at-risk-of-poverty rate (share below 60% of the national median income); **Gini**: Gini coefficient (0 = perfect equality, 1 = perfect inequality); **S80/S20**: income quintile share ratio (top 20% income share divided by bottom 20%).
:::

## Key indicators by year

```{python}
#| label: tbl-poverty
#| tbl-cap: "Poverty and inequality indicators by survey year"
df
```

## Trend analysis

```{python}
#| label: fig-trends
#| fig-cap: "Evolution of the AROP rate, Gini coefficient and S80/S20 ratio"
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(12, 4))
specs = [("AROP%", "AROP rate (%)", "#003399"),
         ("Gini", "Gini coefficient", "#cc3300"),
         ("S80S20", "S80/S20 ratio", "#006633")]
for ax, (col, label, clr) in zip(axes, specs):
    ax.plot(df["Year"], df[col], marker="o", linewidth=2, color=clr)
    ax.fill_between(df["Year"], df[col], alpha=0.10, color=clr)
    ax.set_title(label, fontweight="bold")
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, linestyle="--")
fig.suptitle(f"Poverty & Inequality Trends - {COUNTRY_NAME}", fontweight="bold")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Summary

```{python}
#| output: asis
if len(df):
    first, last = df.iloc[0], df.iloc[-1]
    verb = "rose" if last["AROP%"] > first["AROP%"] else "fell"
    print(f"Between {int(first['Year'])} and {int(last['Year'])}, the "
          f"at-risk-of-poverty rate {verb} from {first['AROP%']}% to "
          f"{last['AROP%']}%, while the Gini coefficient moved from "
          f"{first['Gini']} to {last['Gini']} and the S80/S20 ratio from "
          f"{first['S80S20']} to {last['S80S20']}.")
else:
    print("No data available for this country in the EU-SILC PUF sample.")
```
